refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR contained a phrase 'meet or exceed' which can be simplified to 'achieve' to keep it clear and atomic. The FR focuses solely on the required operational availability, avoiding any implementation details or how to achieve it, thus maintaining solution neutrality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time system health monitoring and fault detection
Design modular redundant subsystems to allow failover
Establish predictive maintenance scheduling based on usage and condition data
Incorporate automated recovery and restart procedures after failure
Define operational policies and safety plans to minimize downtime
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design modular redundant subsystems to allow failover

coupling_check.notes: Selecting modular redundancy supports independence between subsystems, reducing coupling. Care must be taken to isolate shared resources to prevent failure propagation.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of modular subsystems that can be independently controlled and monitored
Risk that shared infrastructure may introduce hidden coupling and reduce overall availability
Operational policies must support and not conflict with redundancy mechanisms
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and automated recovery DPs to complement redundancy and enhance availability assurance.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for implementing modular redundant subsystems to enable failover functionality.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs system status and health data for monitoring redundant subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and tasks to manage subsystem operations and failover triggers.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts related to failover events and subsystem safety status.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication between redundant subsystems and external modules for coordination and failover management.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the failover design is central to the NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Partial containment possible but failover logic would diffuse into other modules managing subsystems.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not subsystem redundancy.
Some overlap with safety alerting, but failover design is broader than safety alone, causing moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of designing modular redundant subsystems for failover, as it centralizes the failover logic and subsystem management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services for safety-related failover alerts may cause interface complexity.
If subsystem hardware is tightly coupled with Vehicle module, some physical implementation details might diffuse outside NGHE Solution.
Network Link interface complexity could increase coupling if not well managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks persist, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate failover control logic (in NGHE Solution) from safety alerting mechanisms (in Safety Services) to improve modular clarity without losing abstraction.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of designing modular redundant subsystems to allow failover directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined operational availability target by providing a logical solution to maintain system operation despite failures, which aligns with the acceptance criterion of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services for safety-related failover alerts may cause interface complexity.
If subsystem hardware is tightly coupled with Vehicle module, some physical implementation details might diffuse outside NGHE Solution.
Network Link interface complexity could increase coupling if not well managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment of the DP with a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating minimal diffusion risk. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the system's operational availability via the Telemetry interface and confirming failover functionality through Tasking commands and SafetyAlert notifications, aligned with the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces supporting monitoring, control, and alerting functions necessary to realize the FR.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate failover control logic (within NGHE Solution) from safety alerting mechanisms (within Safety Services) to reduce interface complexity and improve modular clarity without losing abstraction.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks related to interface complexity and module overlap suggest that the current DP and IM allocation can be improved. Specifically, refining the DP to separate failover control logic from safety alerting mechanisms will reduce interface complexity and improve modular clarity, which is critical for safety and maintainability. This revision will enhance the architectural packaging without losing abstraction and better address the high safety criticality risk.

revise.why: To explicitly separate failover control logic within NGHE Solution from safety alerting mechanisms within Safety Services, reducing interface complexity and improving modular clarity.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR but can be improved for modular clarity.
The NGHE Solution module is the primary IM boundary for the DP with high cohesion and containment.
Safety Services module handles safety-related alerts and may overlap with NGHE Solution in current design.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity due to overlap between failover control and safety alerting.
Diffusion risks if subsystem hardware tightly couples with Vehicle module.
Network Link interface complexity increasing coupling if unmanaged.
evidence_needed: Verification results of failover functionality and safety alerting separation after DP refinement.
Updated containment and diffusion scores post-revision.
Safety analysis confirming reduced risk after separation of control and alerting logic.
